channel.title: Azure News Feed
channel.description: Aggregated daily news from Azure blogs

item.title: Visual Studio Code 1.115
item.description: Learn what's new in Visual Studio Code 1.115 (Insiders) Read the full article
item.dc:creator: Visual Studio Code Team

item.title: Copilot usage metrics now identify active and passive Copilot code review users
item.description: Copilot usage metrics now indicate which users have Copilot code review (CCR) activity, and whether that activity was active or passive. Enterprise and organization admins can see how users engage… The post Copilot usage metrics now identify active and passive Copilot code review users appeared...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: GitHub Copilot CLI combines model families for a second opinion
item.description: Discover how Rubber Duck provides a different perspective to GitHub Copilot CLI. The post GitHub Copilot CLI combines model families for a second opinion appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Nick McKenna

item.title: Announcing the end of support for Node.js 20.x in the Azure SDK for JavaScript
item.description: After July 9, 2026, the Azure SDK for JavaScript will no longer support Node.js 20.x. Upgrade to an Active Node.js Long Term Support (LTS) version to stay secure and up-to-date. The post Announcing the end of support for Node.js 20.x in the Azure SDK for JavaScript appeared first on Azure SDK Blog.
item.dc:creator: Minh-Anh Phan

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Network Security Perimeter for Azure Service Bus
item.description: Network Security Perimeter (NSP) support for Azure Service Bus is now generally available. Network Security Perimeter creates a logical network boundary around your Service Bus namespaces and other Azure PaaS resources, blocking unauthorized public access
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Inside an AI‑enabled device code phishing campaign
item.description: A new wave of device code phishing shows how threat actors are scaling account compromise using AI and end‑to‑end automation. This campaign goes beyond traditional phishing by generating live authentication codes on demand, enabling higher success rates and sustained post‑compromise access. The...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft Defender Security Research Team

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Rule impact analysis on Azure Network Watcher
item.description: Rule impact analysis on Azure Network Watcher enables you to preview the impact of security admin rules before applying them to your environment. This feature helps you validate rule behavior, identify potential conflicts, and ensure that connectivity req
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Storm-1175 focuses gaze on vulnerable web-facing assets in high-tempo Medusa ransomware operations
item.description: The financially motivated cybercriminal threat actor Storm-1175 operates high-velocity ransomware campaigns that weaponize recently disclosed vulnerabilities to obtain initial access, exfiltrate data, and deploy Medusa ransomware. The post Storm-1175 focuses gaze on vulnerable web-facing assets in...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft Threat Intelligence

item.title: MCP Apps on Azure Functions: Quickstart with TypeScript
item.description: Learn how to build and deploy MCP (Model Context Protocol) apps on Azure Functions using TypeScript. This guide covers MCP tools, resources, local development, and serverless deployment with a practical weather app example. The post MCP Apps on Azure Functions: Quickstart with TypeScript appeared...
item.dc:creator: Swapnil Nagar

item.title: Organization runner controls for Copilot cloud agent
item.description: Each time Copilot cloud agent works on a task, it starts a new development environment powered by GitHub Actions. By default, this runs on a standard GitHub-hosted runner, but teams… The post Organization runner controls for Copilot cloud agent appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: GPT-5.1 Codex, GPT-5.1-Codex-Max, and GPT-5.1-Codex-Mini deprecated
item.description: We have deprecated the following models across all GitHub Copilot experiences (including Copilot Chat, inline edits, ask and agent modes, and code completions) on April 1, 2026. Model Deprecation date… The post GPT-5.1 Codex, GPT-5.1-Codex-Max, and GPT-5.1-Codex-Mini deprecated appeared first on...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: The uphill climb of making diff lines performant
item.description: The path to better performance is often found in simplicity. The post The uphill climb of making diff lines performant appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Luke Ghenco

item.title: Organization firewall settings for Copilot cloud agent
item.description: Copilot cloud agent includes a built-in agent firewall to control Copilot’s internet access and help protect against prompt injection and data exfiltration. Until now, the firewall was configured at the… The post Organization firewall settings for Copilot cloud agent appeared first on The GitHub...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Copilot cloud agent signs its commits
item.description: Copilot cloud agent now signs every commit it makes. Signed commits appear as Verified on GitHub, giving you confidence that the commit was genuinely made by the agent and hasn’t… The post Copilot cloud agent signs its commits appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Copilot SDK in public preview
item.description: The GitHub Copilot SDK is now available in public preview. This gives you the building blocks to embed Copilot’s agentic capabilities directly into your own applications, workflows, and platform services.… The post Copilot SDK in public preview appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Copilot usage metrics now includes per-user GitHub Copilot CLI activity in organization reports
item.description: Following our enterprise-level, user-level, and organization-level CLI metrics releases, we’re completing coverage with per-user CLI breakdowns in organization reports. Organization admins can now see which individual users are active on… The post Copilot usage metrics now includes per-user GitHub...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: GitHub Actions: Early April 2026 updates
item.description: This month, GitHub Actions adds entrypoint and command overrides for service containers and new security features including OIDC custom properties and VNET failover. Customizing entrypoints for service containers Many GitHub… The post GitHub Actions: Early April 2026 updates appeared first on The...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Explore union types in C# 15
item.description: C# 15 introduces union types — declare a closed set of case types with implicit conversions and exhaustive pattern matching. Try unions in preview today and see the broader exhaustiveness roadmap. The post Explore union types in C# 15 appeared first on .NET Blog.
item.dc:creator: Bill Wagner

item.title: Aspire Docs in Your Terminal (and Your AI’s Brain)
item.description: Aspire 13.2 ships aspire docs, a CLI for browsing, searching, and reading official aspire.dev docs from your terminal, with the same source of truth available to AI skills and automation. The post Aspire Docs in Your Terminal (and Your AI’s Brain) appeared first on Aspire Blog.
item.dc:creator: David Pine

item.title: Retirement: Python support for Azure App Service and Azure Functions on Windows will be retired on March 31, 2027
item.description: Starting March 31, 2027, Python applications hosted on Azure App Service on Windows and Azure Functions on Windows will no longer run. After this date: Your application configuration and content will remain intact Your apps will stop running
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Retirement: AzureDnsEndpoints for Azure storage accounts will be retired March 2027
item.description: AzureDnsZone endpoints (preview) for Azure Blob storage accounts will enter retirement in March 2027. Customers leveraging AzureDnsZone endpoints (preview) should transition to Standard endpoints for all new and existing blob storage account deployments b
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Threat actor abuse of AI accelerates from tool to cyberattack surface
item.description: Generative AI is upgrading cyberattacks, from 450% higher phishing click‑through rates to industrialized MFA bypass. The post Threat actor abuse of AI accelerates from tool to cyberattack surface appeared first on Microsoft Security Blog.
item.dc:creator: Sherrod DeGrippo

item.title: Cookie-controlled PHP webshells: A stealthy tradecraft in Linux hosting environments
item.description: Cookie-gated PHP webshells use obfuscation, php-fpm execution, and cron-based persistence to evade detection in Linux hosting environments. This post examines how this tradecraft conceals execution behind specially crafted HTTP cookies. The post Cookie-controlled PHP webshells: A stealthy...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft Defender Security Research Team

item.title: GitHub Copilot in Visual Studio — March update
item.description: March 2026 brought a major step forward for GitHub Copilot extensibility in Visual Studio, with custom agents, agent skills, and new tools that make the agent smarter and more capable.… The post GitHub Copilot in Visual Studio — March update appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: The Security tab is now Security & quality
item.description: The top-level Security tab across repositories, organizations, and enterprises has been renamed to Security & quality on github.com. This change restructures the navigation to colocate code quality findings alongside security… The post The Security tab is now Security & quality appeared first on...
item.dc:creator: Allison

item.title: Retirement: Service Fabric support for Windows Server 2022 ends on March 31, 2027 - upgrade to Windows Server 2025
item.description: Service Fabric will end support for all clusters running on Windows Server 2022 on March 31, 2027. Customers must upgrade to Windows Server 2025 by this date to remain in a supported state. This change aligns Service Fabric’s Windows OS image support mode
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Mitigating the Axios npm supply chain compromise
item.description: On March 31, 2026, the popular HTTP client Axios experienced a supply chain attack, causing two newly published npm packages for version updates to download from command and control (C2) that Microsoft Threat Intelligence has attributed to the North Korean state actor Sapphire Sleet. Although the...
item.dc:creator: Microsoft Threat Intelligence and Microsoft Defender Security Research Team

item.title: Retirement: Service Fabric support for Windows Server 2019 ends on March 31, 2027 - upgrade to Windows Server 2025
item.description: Service Fabric will end support for all clusters running on Windows Server 2019 on March 31, 2027. Customers must upgrade to Windows Server 2025 by this date to remain in a supported state. This change aligns Service Fabric’s Windows OS image support mode
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Securing the open source supply chain across GitHub
item.description: Recent attacks on open source focus on exfiltrating secrets; here are the prevention steps you can take today, plus a look at the security capabilities GitHub is working on. The post Securing the open source supply chain across GitHub appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Zachary Steindler

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Unlock Client-Side Configuration at Scale with Azure App Configuration and Azure Front Door
item.description: You can now deliver dynamic configuration directly to client-side applications—securely and at CDN scale—thanks to Azure App Configuration’s integration with Azure Front Door. This delivers new flexibility for modern apps and unlocks a critical capability
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Data Box enhances compliance with automatic Secure Erasure Certificates
item.description: Azure Data Box now automatically generates a downloadable Secure Erasure Certificate for every completed order, providing verification that all data on the device has been securely erased in accordance with NIST 800-88 Revision 2 standards. The certific
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Data Box now supports Azure Files Provisioned v2
item.description: Azure Data Box now supports ingestion of data into Azure Files Provisioned v2 storage accounts. Azure Files Provisioned v2 is a billing model where customers provision and pay for specific capacity, IOPS, and throughput values; giving greater flexibility
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [In development] Public Preview: Azure NetApp Files storage with cool access enhancement
item.description: QoS update with cool access enabled on Premium and Ultra service levels improves how Azure NetApp Files balances performance and cost for mixed hot and cool workloads. Throughput automatically adjusts as data moves to cool storage, maintaining hot‑tier pe
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Retirement: Select Azure AI Language Features
item.description: On March 20, 2029, Microsoft will retire the following eight Azure AI Language features: Key Phrase ExtractionSentiment Analysis and Opinion MiningCustom Text ClassificationConversational Language Understanding (CLU)Custom Question Answering (CQA)Orchestr
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Improving the Markdown Editor for Work Items
item.description: We introduced the Markdown editor in July 2025 to bring Markdown support to large text fields in work items. Since then, we’ve received valuable customer feedback highlighting challenges with the editing experience, particularly when switching in and out of edit mode. Many users found the current...
item.dc:creator: Dan Hellem

item.title: Visual Studio Code 1.114
item.description: Learn what's new in Visual Studio Code 1.114 Read the full article
item.dc:creator: Visual Studio Code Team

item.title: The Polyglot tax – Part 3
item.description: Vectors, Analytics, and the End of ETL Part 3 of 4 – The Multimodal Database Series In Part 1: The Polyglot Tax we laid out the fraud detection scenario: a transaction comes in, and before you approve or deny it you need five checks. Order history (relational). Device fingerprint (JSON)....
item.dc:creator: Aditya Badramraju

item.title: Run multiple agents at once with /fleet in Copilot CLI
item.description: /fleet lets Copilot CLI dispatch multiple agents in parallel. Learn how to write prompts that split work across files, declare dependencies, and avoid common pitfalls. The post Run multiple agents at once with /fleet in Copilot CLI appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
item.dc:creator: Matt Nigh

item.title: 🔥Agentic AI Email Bot on AWS Bedrock AgentCore: Full Architecture and O365 Integration (1/2)🔥
item.description: aka, yes your inbox can have a robot in it now
item.dc:creator: Alex Moore

item.title: Bookmark Studio: evolving bookmarks in Visual Studio
item.description: Bookmarks in Visual Studio have always been a simple, reliable feature. Many developers use them regularly, and over the years we’ve heard consistent feedback from those users. Bookmarks were useful, but there were a few core gaps that kept them from being as effective and relevant as they could...
item.dc:creator: Mads Kristensen

item.title: v1.15.0-beta2
item.description: 1.15.0-beta2 (April 01, 2026) NEW FEATURES: We now produce builds for Windows ARM64 (#32719) You can set a deprecated attribute on variable and output blocks to indicate that they are deprecated. This will produce warnings when passing in a value for a deprecated variable or when referencing a...
item.dc:creator: hc-github-team-es-release-engineering

item.title: Project Nighthawk: A Research Agent Built for Field Engineering
item.description: If you work in field engineering, you know the scenario. A customer is deploying AKS in a regulated environment. They hit an issue during node bootstrapping. They want to know exactly what happens when a node joins the cluster, which components run in which order, and whether the behaviour they’re...
item.dc:creator: Diego Casati, Ray Kao

item.title: Building with Azure Skills
item.description: Part 3 of the Azure Skills Plugin series Previously: How to Install the Azure Plugin You’ve installed the Azure Skills Plugin. The Azure MCP server is running. You have a huge collection of tools and skills at your disposal. So, now what do you actually say to it? This post is a prompt cookbook....
item.dc:creator: Chris Harris

item.title: Retirement: External Data Import & Data Connections in Azure Machine Learning will be retired on September 30, 2026
item.description: Import data from external sources (Preview) – S3, Snowflake, Azure SQL Db along with external Data Connections (Preview) in Azure Machine Learning are being deprecated. These features will no longer be available after September 30, 2026. What this means
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure DevOps March 2026 updates
item.description: The following updates were made to Azure DevOps in March 2026. The Remote MCP Server entered public preview, enabling seamless AI-powered integration with Azure DevOps without requiring a local server. Security improvements included
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Event Grid provides new capabilities for smarter, more secure event-driven architectures
item.description: Azure Event Grid offers a variety of features in Public Preview – intended to help you better manage large-scale, distributed systems with improved interoperability and control. Public Preview Features: Cross-Tenant Webhook Delivery: Enables secu
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: User delegation SAS for Azure Tables, Azure Files, and Azure Queues
item.description: User delegation SAS for Azure Tables, Azure Files, and Azure Queues is now generally available. User delegation SAS allows users to create a more secure SAS token than account or service SAS by tying the SAS token to the delegator. This extension of UDK
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Event Grid provides new capabilities for smarter, more secure event-driven architectures
item.description: Azure Event Grid now offers a variety of new features intended to help you better manage large-scale, distributed systems with improved interoperability and control. Generally Available features: MQTT Message Ordering: Ensures in-order message de
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: [Launched] Generally Available: Microsoft Azure now available from new cloud region in Denmark
item.description: Microsoft announces the opening of its cloud region in Denmark to accelerate digital transformation and AI innovation. The Denmark East region provides Danish customers with local, secure cloud infrastructure, supporting local data residency, low latency,
item.dc:creator: Microsoft

item.title: Aspire 13.2: Dashboard Gets Smarter Export and Telemetry
item.description: Aspire 13.2 brings powerful new dashboard features for exporting and importing telemetry, querying telemetry via API, and improved GenAI telemetry visualization. The post Aspire 13.2: Dashboard Gets Smarter Export and Telemetry appeared first on Aspire Blog.
item.dc:creator: James Newton-King
